metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Claude Code can naturally produce huge JSONL lines — a single tool_result block can contain MBs of stdout, a base64 artifact, or a large aggregated diff. From the agent's side there is nothing to 'support': the transcript flusher writes whatever the tool returned, terminated by `\n`. So agent_supports is trivially yes.

The spec's observable is parser resilience: the session must arc `ready → working → ready` and NOT stick in `working` because the parser stalled on a giant line.

irrlicht's tailer was explicitly hardened for this (issue #270). `core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go` uses `bufio.NewReaderSize(file, 64*1024)` plus a custom `readLineCapped` rather than `bufio.Scanner` — Scanner's hard ~2 MB cap would have wedged on big tool outputs. The cap `maxTranscriptLineSize` is 64 MB; lines beyond that are logged ('skipping oversized line at offset N') and the file offset is advanced past them so processing continues with the next line. There is also an `errPartialAtEOF` path so a partially-written giant line is re-read once more bytes arrive.

For the claudecode JSON path specifically: huge tool_result content lives inside `message.content[].content` strings. Those are surfaced as `ev.ToolResultIDs` / `ev.IsError` at parse time, and only `ExtractContentChars` / `ExtractAssistantText` touch large strings — both are bounded scans over the decoded map. The subsequent assistant text + turn_done will then drive the working→ready transition normally. So `core/pkg/tailer/large_line_test.go` already pins the regression (it asserts that a line above the old Scanner cap doesn't block the tailer, and that subsequent lines still parse).

Net: oversized line either fits under 64 MB (parsed normally) or is skipped with a log line; either way the turn arc completes.
caveats: Lines above the 64 MB cap are dropped silently from the parser's point of view — they still advance the file offset, but their content (tool result, etc.) is lost. The spec only asserts the state arc, not the content, so this is fine for the cell; it's a known operator-visible-log-only behaviour.

Reliably manufacturing a >2 MB JSONL line from a real `claude` CLI driver is awkward — the recipe likely synthesizes the oversized line by appending hand-crafted bytes into the live transcript file rather than coaxing Claude to emit one. Either approach exercises the same tailer path.

sources.note: bufio.NewReaderSize + readLineCapped + maxTranscriptLineSize=64MB; oversized lines logged and skipped past, not stalled (see comment block referencing #270).
Regression test for #270 — asserts that a JSONL line above bufio.Scanner's old 2 MB cap does not block the tailer and that subsequent lines parse.
Oversized JSONL line wedge fixed by switching tailer from bufio.Scanner to bufio.Reader + readLineCapped.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/

script.text: Write a single very long sentence on one line. Make it at least 5000 words. Do not use newlines except at the end.

verify: events.jsonl shows the UUID session entering ready after launch
events.jsonl shows exactly one ready→working transition for the UUID
events.jsonl shows exactly one working→ready transition at turn end (completion of the arc is the parser-resilience proof; if the tailer wedged on the long line this transition would never fire)
irrlichd logs do NOT contain a 'skipping oversized line' warning for the UUID's transcript (a 5000-word line is well under the 64 MB cap)
transcript.jsonl contains 1 user line and at least 1 assistant line; the assistant line is a single long JSONL row with no embedded newlines in message.content
